wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the project overview section focused on recall - a production-ready, mobile-first web application designed to transform PDFs into smart flashcards using AI-powered spaced repetition. Explain the application's purpose, core value proposition, and target audience. Document the key features including PDF-to-flashcard conversion, AI-powered content generation, spaced repetition system, and analytics dashboard. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the complete workflow from PDF upload to study sessions. Document the technology stack, architectural approach, and design philosophy. Include system context diagrams showing how different components interact.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the recall application focused on rapid onboarding and initial setup. Explain the prerequisites including Node.js, Git, and Supabase account requirements. Document the complete setup process from repository cloning through database configuration to running the development server. Include step-by-step instructions for environment variable configuration, database migration, and first-time deployment. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues. Make the content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while ensuring all critical setup steps are covered. Include verification steps to confirm successful installation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for the recall application. Explain the Next.js 14 App Router structure, server components pattern implementation, and component hierarchy. Document the layout system including RootLayout, AppLayout, Sidebar, and TopBar components. Detail the responsive design approach using Tailwind CSS, font management with Inter font, and viewport configuration. Explain the component composition patterns, prop drilling prevention, and state management integration. Include performance optimizations like server-side rendering, static generation, and image optimization. Document the routing strategy, dynamic routes for decks and study sessions, and navigation patterns. Address accessibility considerations, SEO optimization, and cross-browser compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: PDF Upload and Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PDF upload and processing pipeline. Explain the complete workflow from file upload through text extraction to flashcard generation. Document the multipart form handling, file validation (size limits, type restrictions), and streaming response implementation. Detail the PDF parsing process using pdf-parse, text extraction techniques, and content chunking strategy. Explain the rate limiting mechanism, error handling patterns, and progress reporting system. Include practical examples of the upload flow, configuration options, and troubleshooting common issues like unsupported file types or large files. Address performance considerations and memory management during processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive schema overview documentation for the Prisma database schema. Explain the overall database architecture, entity relationships between Deck, Card, and ReviewLog models. Document the Prisma configuration including datasource setup, generator settings, and PostgreSQL provider configuration. Detail the ID generation strategy using cuid(), timestamps management, and automatic updatedAt fields. Include entity relationship diagrams showing foreign key relationships and cascade deletion policies. Explain the database URL configuration through environment variables and connection management. Document the migration system and initial schema setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Upload API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the upload endpoint that processes PDF files and generates flashcards. Document the POST /api/upload endpoint including multipart/form-data requirements, file validation rules, and streaming response format. Detail the PDF processing pipeline including text extraction, chunking, AI generation, and database storage. Include request parameters (file, title, subject), response format with progress updates, error handling for various failure scenarios, and rate limiting behavior. Provide cURL examples and client integration patterns for handling the streaming response.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the layout components system. Detail the AppLayout component's conditional rendering logic for study vs. dashboard views, sidebar navigation structure, top bar functionality, and responsive design patterns. Document the Sidebar component's menu items, user authentication integration, and mobile responsiveness. Explain the TopBar component's search functionality, user controls, and notification system. Cover PageWrapper for content wrapping and LandingHero for landing page hero sections. Include component props, state management, styling with Tailwind CSS, and integration patterns with Next.js routing.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Integration and Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AI integration customization in recall. Detail the OpenRouter API integration architecture, custom AI provider implementation patterns, and prompt engineering strategies. Document the Flashcard interface, ProgressCallback types, and AI generation pipeline. Explain how to add new AI providers, configure model fallback strategies, and customize system prompts. Include examples of extending the generateFlashcardsFromChunk function, implementing custom deduplication logic, and handling different AI response formats. Address rate limiting, error handling, and performance optimization for AI integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deck Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Deck model entity. Detail all fields including id (String, @id, @default(cuid())), title (String), description (String?), subject (String?), emoji (String, @default('🧠')), cardCount (Int, @default(0)), createdAt (DateTime, @default(now())), updatedAt (DateTime, @updatedAt), and lastStudiedAt (DateTime?). Explain the model's relationships with Card[] and ReviewLog[]. Document field constraints, default values, and validation rules. Include Prisma client usage patterns for CRUD operations, query methods, and relationship queries. Provide examples of deck creation, updates, and retrieval operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: PDF Upload and Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the PDF upload and processing feature focused on the complete workflow from file upload to processed content. Document the upload interface implementation, drag-and-drop functionality, file validation, and processing pipeline. Explain the PDF parsing process using pdf-parse, text extraction techniques, and content preprocessing. Detail the chunking strategy for large documents, error handling mechanisms, and progress tracking. Include practical examples of supported file formats, size limitations, and processing time expectations. Address user experience considerations, accessibility features, and integration with the AI generation system. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common upload issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for recall application. Cover prerequisites including Node.js, Git, and Supabase account setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions from cloning the repository to running the development server. Document the critical database setup requirements using Supabase PostgreSQL, explaining why SQLite doesn't work on Vercel. Include environment variable configuration, dependency installation, database migration process, and initial application launch. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues. Include verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the key features section focused on recall's core functionality and capabilities. Document the PDF-to-flashcard conversion process, AI-powered content generation, spaced repetition system implementation, and study interface features. Explain how each feature works, its benefits, and integration with other system components. Include practical examples demonstrating the complete workflow from PDF upload through study sessions. Address user experience aspects, performance characteristics, and customization options. Make content accessible to both end users and developers by explaining both functional and technical aspects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backend architecture documentation for the recall application's API layer. Explain the Next.js API route handler pattern implementation, request/response processing workflows, and middleware integration. Document the authentication middleware using Supabase, protected route handling, and session management. Detail the database abstraction layer using Prisma ORM, connection pooling, and transaction management. Explain the serverless deployment model on Vercel, cold start optimization, and scaling considerations. Document error handling strategies, logging patterns, and monitoring approaches. Include security measures like input validation, CORS configuration, and rate limiting. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI-Powered Flashcard Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the AI-powered flashcard generation system. Explain the integration with OpenRouter/OpenAI APIs, prompt engineering strategies, and content processing pipeline. Document the subject classification system, emoji mapping, and content chunk processing. Detail the streaming progress reporting, error handling for API failures, and rate limiting responses. Include examples of prompt templates, content filtering mechanisms, and duplicate detection algorithms. Explain the fallback strategies for API unavailability and provide guidance on optimizing prompt quality and cost efficiency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for each entity in the database schema. Document the Deck model with fields like id, title, description, subject, emoji, cardCount, timestamps, and lastStudiedAt. Detail the Card model including deckId relation, front/back content, difficulty levels, status tracking, spaced repetition fields (easeFactor, interval, repetitionCount), scheduling fields (nextReviewAt, lastReviewedAt), and timestamps. Explain the ReviewLog model with cardId and deckId relations, rating system, and reviewedAt timestamps. Document field constraints, default values, data types, and validation rules. Include Prisma client usage patterns and model relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deck Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for deck management endpoints including GET /api/decks/[id] for retrieving deck details and cards, and GET /api/decks/minimal for lightweight deck listings. Document request parameters, response schemas for full and minimal deck representations, pagination options, and filtering capabilities. Include authentication requirements, error responses for missing decks, and performance considerations for large decks. Provide examples of typical use cases like deck browsing, card retrieval, and bulk operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for feature-specific components that implement core application functionality. Document the DeckBrowser component's grid/list view switching, filtering capabilities, and infinite scrolling. Explain DeckCard component's interactive elements, hover effects, and navigation triggers. Detail DeckDetailCardList's card management features, bulk operations, and editing capabilities. Cover DeckGrid component's responsive grid layout and card arrangement. Document Flashcard3D component's 3D animation system, flip mechanics, and user interaction handling. Explain StudySessionShell's study session orchestration, timing controls, and progress tracking. Include component composition patterns, prop interfaces, state management, and integration with the spaced repetition system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extensibility Patterns and Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for extensibility patterns and customization points in recall. Document component extension mechanisms, custom component creation patterns, and API endpoint customization. Explain how to extend the spaced repetition system, add new deck subjects, and customize the study interface. Include guidance for creating custom UI components, extending the Prisma schema, and implementing new study modes. Address plugin architectures, configuration management, and maintaining backward compatibility when extending functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Card Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Card model entity. Document all fields including id (String, @id, @default(cuid())), deckId (String) with cascade deletion relation to Deck, front/back content (String), difficulty (String, @default('MEDIUM')), status (String, @default('NEW')), spaced repetition fields (easeFactor: Float @default(2.5), interval: Int @default(0), repetitionCount: Int @default(0)), scheduling fields (nextReviewAt: DateTime @default(now()), lastReviewedAt: DateTime?), and timestamps (createdAt, updatedAt). Explain the SM-2 algorithm integration through easeFactor and interval fields. Detail the relationship with Deck and ReviewLog. Include Prisma client usage patterns for card operations, spaced repetition calculations, and review scheduling.

wiki_overview.content:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me create the analysis.

<blog>

# Cumaths: Smart Flashcard Learning Platform

## Project Introduction

**Cumaths** is an intelligent flashcard learning application built with Next.js 14 that transforms PDF documents into personalized flashcards using AI-powered generation. The platform implements spaced repetition algorithms to optimize learning efficiency and provides an immersive study experience through interactive 3D flashcards.

### Purpose Statement
The application aims to revolutionize educational content consumption by automatically converting complex PDF materials into digestible flashcards, enabling students to master subjects through active recall and spaced repetition techniques.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **AI-Powered Content Generation**: Automatically extract key concepts from PDFs using OpenAI
- **Spaced Repetition System**: Implement SM-2 algorithm for optimal learning scheduling
- **Immersive Learning Experience**: Provide engaging 3D flashcard interactions
- **Performance Optimization**: Ensure smooth, responsive user experience across devices

### Target Audience
- Students preparing for exams
- Lifelong learners seeking efficient study methods
- Educators looking for innovative teaching tools
- Anyone seeking to improve retention through spaced repetition

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern full-stack architecture built on Next.js 14 with Server Components and Edge Runtime capabilities.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[API Routes]
    C --> D[Prisma ORM]
    D --> E[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    
    C --> F[AI Integration]
    F --> G[OpenAI API]
    
    B --> H[React Components]
    H --> I[3D Flashcard System]
    H --> J[Study Interface]
    H --> K[Deck Management]
    
    L[Supabase Auth] --> C
    M[Middleware] --> B
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**:
- **App Shell**: Layout system with sidebar navigation and top bar
- **Study Interface**: Interactive flashcard system with 3D animations
- **Deck Management**: Browse, create, and organize flashcard collections
- **Upload System**: PDF processing pipeline with real-time progress feedback

**Backend Layer**:
- **API Routes**: REST endpoints for CRUD operations and business logic
- **Database Layer**: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM for data persistence
- **AI Integration**: OpenAI-powered content extraction and flashcard generation
- **Authentication**: Supabase authentication integration

**Data Flow Architecture**:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant UI as Frontend
    participant API as API Route
    participant DB as Database
    participant AI as OpenAI
    
    U->>UI: Upload PDF
    UI->>API: POST /api/upload
    API->>AI: Extract content
    AI-->>API: Structured content
    API->>AI: Generate flashcards
    AI-->>API: Flashcard pairs
    API->>DB: Save deck and cards
    DB-->>API: Confirmation
    API-->>UI: Progress updates
    UI-->>U: Completion notification
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Shell**: `src/app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with metadata and theme configuration
- **Study Interface**: `src/app/decks/[id]/study/page.tsx` - Dynamic study session routing
- **Upload Pipeline**: `src/app/upload/page.tsx` - Client-side upload and processing interface

### Core Modules

**Database Layer** (`src/lib/db.ts`):
- Prisma Client initialization with environment-aware configuration
- Production-ready database URL selection with SSL enforcement
- Connection pooling optimization for serverless environments

**Spaced Repetition Engine** (`src/lib/spaced-repetition.ts`):
- Full SM-2 algorithm implementation for learning optimization
- Card queue management with due date prioritization
- Performance-optimized card selection for study sessions

**AI Integration** (`src/lib/ai.ts`):
- PDF parsing and text extraction
- OpenAI API integration for flashcard generation
- Content chunking and deduplication strategies

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: Database URLs, AI API keys, and feature flags
- **Prisma Schema**: Type-safe database modeling with relations
- **Next.js Config**: Build optimization and runtime settings

### External Dependencies
- **@prisma/client**: Database ORM with type safety
- **@supabase/supabase-js**: Authentication and real-time features
- **openai**: AI-powered content generation
- **framer-motion**: Smooth animations and transitions
- **pdf-parse**: PDF text extraction

### Integration Points
- **Supabase**: Authentication, real-time features, and edge functions
- **OpenAI**: Content analysis and flashcard generation
- **PostgreSQL**: Relational data storage with Prisma
- **Edge Runtime**: Serverless execution for improved performance

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[AppLayout] --> B[Sidebar]
    A --> C[TopBar]
    A --> D[Main Content]
    
    D --> E[DeckBrowser]
    D --> F[DeckDetail]
    D --> G[StudySession]
    
    G --> H[Flashcard3D]
    G --> I[RatingSystem]
    
    E --> J[DeckCard]
    F --> K[DeckDetailCardList]
    
    L[UploadPage] --> M[DropZone]
    L --> N[ProcessingUI]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Smart Flashcard Generation**:
- AI-powered content extraction from PDFs
- Automatic flashcard creation with question-answer pairs
- Subject-based categorization and emoji assignment

**Spaced Repetition System**:
- SM-2 algorithm implementation for optimal learning scheduling
- Dynamic card queuing based on due dates and difficulty
- Progress tracking and mastery visualization

**Interactive Study Experience**:
- 3D flip animations with smooth transitions
- Keyboard shortcuts for efficient studying
- Real-time progress tracking and statistics

### Implementation Highlights

**Upload Pipeline** (`src/app/api/upload/route.ts`):
- Streaming progress updates during PDF processing
- Rate limiting and validation for robust operation
- Deduplication and quality filtering for reliable results

**Study Interface** (`src/components/flashcard/StudySessionShell.tsx`):
- Optimistic UI updates for responsive interactions
- Confetti animations for achievement feedback
- Comprehensive keyboard accessibility support

**Data Persistence** (`prisma/schema.prisma`):
- Entity relationships with proper constraints
- Automatic timestamps and audit trails
- Efficient indexing for common queries

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Uploading: Upload PDF
    Uploading --> Processing: Parse PDF
    Processing --> Generating: Extract Content
    Generating --> Saving: Create Flashcards
    Saving --> Complete: Database Write
    Complete --> Idle: Reset
    
    Idle --> Studying: Start Session
    Studying --> Rating: Show Card
    Rating --> Studying: Next Card
    Rating --> Complete: Session End
```

The application demonstrates excellent architectural decisions including:
- **Separation of Concerns**: Clear division between frontend, backend, and AI layers
- **Performance Optimization**: Streaming responses, client-side caching, and efficient database queries
- **User Experience**: Responsive design, smooth animations, and intuitive interactions
- **Scalability**: Serverless-first approach with Prisma for database abstraction

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [AppLayout.tsx](src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx)
- [db.ts](src/lib/db.ts)
- [schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [spaced-repetition.ts](src/lib/spaced-repetition.ts)
- [review/route.ts](src/app/api/review/route.ts)
- [page.tsx](src/app/decks/[id]/page.tsx)
- [Flashcard3D.tsx](src/components/flashcard/Flashcard3D.tsx)
- [study/page.tsx](src/app/decks/[id]/study/page.tsx)
- [StudySessionShell.tsx](src/components/flashcard/StudySessionShell.tsx)
- [constants.ts](src/lib/constants.ts)
- [upload/page.tsx](src/app/upload/page.tsx)
- [upload/route.ts](src/app/api/upload/route.ts)

</blog>
